title: inula persica
intro: Inula persica, also known as Persian mallow or Platanus persica, is a flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to Iran and other parts of the Middle East. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Inula persica include reducing inflammation and pain, as well as treating digestive issues and respiratory problems. Inula persica can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a versatile and accessible medicinal plant for various health applications.
